# Data Primacy

## The Physics

Data is the most valuable asset in any organization. Processes can be rebuilt.
People can be retrained. Tools can be replaced. But lost data is gone forever,
and corrupted data produces corrupted decisions at every level it touches.

Data has a quality hierarchy: raw observations become structured information,
which becomes governed knowledge, which becomes applied wisdom. Each level
requires deliberate transformation — data does not improve by accident.

## Why It Matters

- **Decisions depend on data**: Every decision is only as good as the data informing it.
- **Compounding damage**: Corrupt data at the base corrupts every downstream conclusion.
- **Irreversibility**: Lost data cannot be reconstructed from its derivatives.
- **Governance foundation**: You cannot govern what you cannot measure, and measurement IS data.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- DIKW hierarchy (Ackoff, 1989): Data → Information → Knowledge → Wisdom.
- Data mesh principles (Dehghani): data as a product, with ownership and quality standards.
- GIGO (Garbage In, Garbage Out): foundational computing principle.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-C06 (Documentation Discipline) — the practice of capturing data
- JOS-C07 (Knowledge Decay) — data quality degrades without maintenance
- JOS-P05 (Source of Truth) — one authoritative data source per domain
